title: New query parameter for custom URLs!
excerpt: We just added the section query parameter that enables you to create a custom URL that opens a specific section of your app listing page. Your custom URL can open directly on the Overview, Pricing, Security & Compliance,…

posts.author: rachelatmonday (Rachel)
posts.cookedHtml:
We just added the section query parameter that enables you to create a custom URL that opens a specific section of your app listing page. Your custom URL can open directly on the Overview, Pricing, Security & Compliance, or Permissions tab.

Check out our documentation for examples!

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
Nice!

Though I already found a documentation note! (shocking that I’m the one to report it, right?)

The App listing page samples show <app_id> when it is the marketplace listing ID which is different (for whatever reason) from the app_id which is used in the installed_apps pages (which is the app_id used everywhere except the marketplace listing).
